title: Arthur Sellers

infobox.value: This biographical article related to an English cricket person born in the 1870s is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Arthur_Sellers&action=edit).

(31 May 1870 – 25 September 1941) was an English [amateur](/wiki/Amateur_status_in_first-class_cricket "Amateur status in first-class cricket") [first-class cricketer](/wiki/First-class_cricket "First-class cricket"), who played for [Yorkshire County Cricket Club](/wiki/Yorkshire_County_Cricket_Club "Yorkshire County Cricket Club") from 1890 to 1899,[[1]](#cite_note-YB-1) and in other first-class matches for the North of England (1893) and Gentlemen of England (1895).

Warner, David (2011). *The Yorkshire County Cricket Club: 2011 Yearbook* (113th ed.). Ilkley, Yorkshire: Great Northern Books. p. 377. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-905080-85-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-905080-85-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1-905080-85-4").

first_paragraph: Not to be confused with [Arthur Sellers (Scottish cricketer)](/wiki/Arthur_Sellers_(Scottish_cricketer) "Arthur Sellers (Scottish cricketer)").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English cricketer

Not to be confused with [Arthur Sellers (Scottish cricketer)](/wiki/Arthur_Sellers_(Scottish_cricketer) "Arthur Sellers (Scottish cricketer)").

Arthur Sellers

**Arthur Sellers** (31 May 1870 – 25 September 1941) was an English [amateur](/wiki/Amateur_status_in_first-class_cricket "Amateur status in first-class cricket") [first-class cricketer](/wiki/First-class_cricket "First-class cricket"), who played for [Yorkshire County Cricket Club](/wiki/Yorkshire_County_Cricket_Club "Yorkshire County Cricket Club") from 1890 to 1899,[[1]](#cite_note-YB-1) and in other first-class matches for the North of England (1893) and Gentlemen of England (1895).

Born in [Keighley](/wiki/Keighley "Keighley"), [Yorkshire](/wiki/Yorkshire "Yorkshire"), Sellers, in 53 first-class games, scored 1,852 runs at 19.91, hitting two centuries against [Middlesex](/wiki/Middlesex_County_Cricket_Club "Middlesex County Cricket Club") and [Somerset](/wiki/Somerset_County_Cricket_Club "Somerset County Cricket Club"). Sellers took 47 catches and two wickets, at 74.50. Both victims came in a spell of 2 for 28 against [Kent](/wiki/Kent_County_Cricket_Club "Kent County Cricket Club").

Sellers died in Keighley in September 1941.

His son, [Brian Sellers](/wiki/Brian_Sellers "Brian Sellers"), was Yorkshire [captain](/wiki/Captain_(cricket) "Captain (cricket)") from 1933 to 1947, and a [Wisden Cricketer of the Year](/wiki/Wisden_Cricketer_of_the_Year "Wisden Cricketer of the Year") in 1940.
